evidence_excerpt: Hypoxia improved after removal of casts... The patient had no further exacerbations from plastic bronchitis during the rest of the pregnancy and had a successfully delivery at 37 weeks. During the second bronchoscopy the cast had recurred in the left main bronchus.

quality_of_life.functional_status_evidence_excerpt: No further exacerbations from plastic bronchitis during the rest of the pregnancy and successfully delivered at 37 weeks (suggesting daily activities generally preserved).
quality_of_life.symptom_burden_evidence_excerpt: The patient had no further exacerbations from plastic bronchitis during the rest of the pregnancy.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: She developed hypoxia with oxygen saturation dropping to 90% requiring 100% FiO2; intubation was performed.
Hypoxia improved after removal of casts.
Bronchoscopy was repeated to remove residual cast; endobronchial N-acetylcystine was used as an adjunct.
The patient had no further exacerbations from plastic bronchitis during the rest of the pregnancy and had a successfully delivery at 37 weeks.

notes_on_uncertainty: Follow-up is limited to the remainder of pregnancy (~6 months). No data beyond delivery; long-term (>12 months) status is unknown. Functional status and symptom burden are inferred from the absence of further exacerbations and successful progression to term.
